text:
Market today: Retail sentiment is turning negative, with global ripple effects. This could weigh on your tech/chip heavy portfolio, despite some positive AI news. SpaceX issues add to a cautious tech mood.

Your holdings: SOXL (-44.02%) and SPAL (-41.51%) are your biggest concerns, both leveraged and deeply underwater. QQQ (-1.22%), SMH (-10.6%), and QQQI (-2.52%) are also flagged for trimming. Margin amplifies these drops significantly.

Watch: Keep a close eye on retail investor sentiment and any further weakness in leveraged tech/chip ETFs. Also, monitor the broader AI sector for follow-through on new developments.

Bottom line: Prioritize aggressive risk management on your leveraged and deeply negative positions.
plays: SOXL: Your scanner flags "SELL" and it's down 44%. Consider cutting this leveraged position to reduce significant margin risk.
SPAL: This leveraged ETF is also down 41%. Re-evaluate its stop or consider trimming to manage exposure.
NVDA: Scanner shows a "BUY" setup (9/12) at 206.55-206.25, stop 205.54. If reallocating, this chip name offers a potential entry.
